preamble: The shift from the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] to a retroflex stop [ɖ] (or retroflex approximant [ɻ]) is a documented phonetic development in specific English and Scandinavian dialects, where posterior rhotics are replaced by retracted articulations.

examples.note: Documentation of the 'Northumbrian Burr' (uvular R) shifting toward or being replaced by retroflex articulations in modern varieties.
In some southern dialects, historical uvular rhotics interact with following dental sounds to produce retroflex stops (e.g., /rt/ > [ʈ], /rd/ > [ɖ]).

sources: Svantesson, J. O. (2001). The uvular rhotic in Southern Swedish.
